attack_surface.exposure: code.load → app.types.Loader.Register scans an Execute-gated assembly for [PlangType] classes and ITypeRenderer implementations; runtime registrations win over generator-emitted entries in ResolveType + renderers.Of.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: FilePath.AuthGate(Execute) on the DLL path. Past that, every code path is user-sovereign per the PLang threat model.
mitigations: SealedNames allowlist refuses to shadow identity/signature/signedoperation/callback/channel at all three gate sites (explicit [PlangType], inferred @this name, ITypeRenderer.TypeName) — case-insensitive OrdinalIgnoreCase
renderer ctor wrapped in try/catch except OOM/SOF
Coverage check: every newly-registered [PlangType] needs ≥1 renderer or load fails with TypeLoadCoverage
Tester v5 mutation-confirmed each sealed-gate site by neutralising it independently
gaps: Loader.Register bails on the FIRST sealed-name collision; PlangType registrations from preceding classes in the same DLL stay in _runtimeNameToType (no rollback). Sub-Low informational — the user already crossed the Execute trust boundary, so a hostile DLL's non-sealed registrations would have succeeded anyway under by-design accepted-risk.

attack_surface.exposure: app.types.image.@this constructed from bytes via file.read MIME=image/*, image.ResolveAsync (any path scheme), FromDataUrl base64, bare base64. Dimensions read lazily via SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image.Identify(Bytes).
attack_surface.trust_boundary: file.read/HTTP go through path.@this AuthGate. image.ResolveAsync delegates to path.@this.Resolve.
mitigations: ImageSharp.Image.Identify is header-only (does not decode pixel data)
gaps: No max-bytes cap on ReadBytes (existing standing finding; now extends to image type)
No shipping handler binds Data<image> from string today — ResolveAsync HTTP branch is latent

attack_surface.exposure: All math handlers (add, subtract, multiply, divide, intdiv, modulo, power, abs, floor, ceiling, round, sqrt, min, max) now route through number.* methods. Each number.* wraps in number.Wrap which catches DivideByZero, Overflow, Arithmetic, PowerExponentTooLarge.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Action parameters are typed (Data<number>, Data<int>). Source is action-call site arguments (LLM-produced .pr literals, %var% values).
mitigations: Wrap envelope covers the four arithmetic exception families with typed Data.Fail keys
PowerExponentTooLarge cap = 64 protects three CPU-loop branches (Tester v5 mutation-confirmed)
gaps: Wrap does NOT catch ArgumentOutOfRangeException — System.Math.Round(decimal, decimals) throws when decimals∉[0,28] (or [0,15] for double); math.round Decimals=%var% can land any int. Same shape applies to any future call with similarly-bounded framework parameters.

attack_surface.exposure: Wire.Read processes incoming Data wire shape; LiftDataIfShaped restarts STJ for sub-objects; Normalize tags registered-PlangType values as TypedValueNode; json.Writer routes them via renderers.Of(typeName, format).
attack_surface.trust_boundary: All incoming Data crosses Wire before signing.verify gate.
mitigations: AsyncLocal _readDepth counter capped at MaxReadDepth=64 (v1 fix on builder-ergonomics, regression suite intact)
json.Writer fail-closed default (throws NormalizeUnexpectedLeafType for any value not in the tree contract)
RendererLookupMissed thrown when a tagged TypedValueNode has no renderer

attack_surface.exposure: number.@this.Parse(string) and Resolve(string, context) accept arbitrary strings; arithmetic surface routes through DoOp/DoPower/Do*; policy axes resolved via MathPolicy.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Source is action handler parameters; bound by typed signature.
mitigations: Overflow / DivideByZero / Arithmetic wrapped in typed Data.Fail keys

findings.vector: math.power exponent unbounded — `for (long i = 0; i < expL; i++)` in DoPower at PLang/app/types/number/this.Arithmetic.cs with no upper bound below long.MaxValue.
findings.preconditions: User script calls math.power with attacker-influenced exponent (LLM JSON / HTTP / file content reaching %Exp%).
findings.impact: Local CPU DoS — synchronous spin within actor process.
findings.proposed_fix: Cap |expL| at Config.MaxPowerExponent (default 64), typed PowerExponentTooLarge failure.
findings.fix_landed: coder v4 (8c99912a0) + codeanalyzer-driven rework moved cap into the three CPU-loop branches via EnsureExponentInRange; Math.Pow branches deliberately skip. Tester v5 mutation-confirmed coverage at each loop site and skip at each Math.Pow site.

findings.vector: Loader.Register lets a third-party DLL shadow built-in [PlangType] names like 'identity' via runtime-wins precedence — outer Data.EnsureSigned then signs an attacker-composed body with the actor's legitimate key.
findings.impact: Authentically-signed envelopes whose body is renderer-controlled. Accepted-risk under user-sovereign model but trust transfer is non-obvious.
findings.exploit_sketch: Hostile DLL declares [PlangType('identity')] + ITypeRenderer{TypeName='identity'}; Loader.Register replaces routing; subsequent Identity-typed Data emits attacker-controlled body, gets signed by real actor key.
findings.proposed_fix: SealedNames allowlist of signing/transport-load-bearing names refused at all three gate sites.
findings.fix_landed: coder v4 (8c99912a0) added SealedNames = {identity, signature, signedoperation, callback, channel} (OrdinalIgnoreCase). Coder v5 (1cdb0a840) added two missing gate sites tester v4 flagged. Tester v5 mutation-confirmed each site fails independently when its check is neutralised.

findings.vector: image.@this byte intake has no max-bytes cap. file.read constructs image.@this from any image/* MIME read result without a size guard; image.ResolveAsync's HTTP-URL branch calls path.ReadBytes() with no cap. byte[] is the OOM vector; ImageSharp Identify is header-only and safe.
findings.preconditions: (a) `read %image%` against a large file/HTTP URL with AuthGate(Read) approved, OR (b) future handler exposes Data<image> with string input.
findings.impact: Actor-process OOM, local DoS today; flips channel-shaped when any Data<image> string-binding handler ships.
findings.exploit_sketch: `- read https://attacker/huge.jpg`. AuthGate(HttpRead) approved → ReadBytes returns multi-GB → image.@this stores reference → memory residency until variable cleared.
findings.proposed_fix: Consolidate ReadBytes(maxBytes?) at the path-verb layer with ImageTooLarge typed failure. Covers this finding + the standing OpenAiProvider ReadAllBytes case.
findings.deferral_note: coder v4 deferred — no shipping handler binds Data<image> from string today; fix is architectural (path-verb layer) and best landed in a focused 'path-verb size guards' branch.

findings.vector: math.round with Decimals out of [0, 28] (decimal) or [0, 15] (double) throws ArgumentOutOfRangeException from System.Math.Round. number.Wrap catches DivideByZero / Overflow / Arithmetic / PowerExponentTooLarge but NOT ArgumentOutOfRangeException — the exception escapes the action handler instead of surfacing as a typed Data.Fail.
findings.preconditions: User script feeds attacker-influenced int into Decimals slot: `- round %x% Decimals=%user_decimals%`. Default is 0, but the slot accepts any int.
findings.impact: Uncaught runtime exception out of the action handler. Generic runtime-error surface instead of typed Data.Fail — degrades the error-key contract that downstream goals branch on. Not a crash escalation, but breaks the action-boundary discipline the rest of the math family observes.
findings.exploit_sketch: `- round %value% Decimals=999, on error set %err% = true` — under the existing typed-failure shape the Wrap envelope would map to ArithmeticError; today it throws ArgumentOutOfRangeException uncaught. Downstream `%err%` may not bind because the failure shape isn't the one the on-error clause expects.
findings.proposed_fix: Either (a) clamp decimals into the framework-legal range at DoRound entry (preserve the typed-failure contract, surface ArithmeticError for out-of-range), OR (b) add ArgumentException to Wrap's catch list with a typed key like 'InvalidArgument'. Option (a) is the smaller change. Same shape should be applied wherever a framework call has bounded-int parameters; the math family is the immediate locus.

summary: PASS. v1 F1 (math.power exponent cap) and F2 (Loader sealed-names allowlist) are both closed with mutation-confirmed coverage at all gate sites — coder v4 + v5 cycle plus tester v5 mutation-tested each independently. New Low finding F4: math.round throws uncaught ArgumentOutOfRangeException on Decimals∉[0,28]/[0,15], degrading the action-boundary typed-failure contract. F3 (image byte cap) remains a deliberate deferral — latent, no shipping handler exposes Data<image> from string, fix is architectural (path-verb layer) and slated for a separate branch. Wire/Normalize/json.Writer integration remains solid; v1 builder-ergonomics depth-guard intact. Semgrep architectural baseline unchanged at 15 (all known serializer-hygiene). Branch is safe to merge with F3 + F4 tracked.
